repo.description: Vim-fork focused on extensibility and usability
repo.name: neovim

body:
(Comments welcome, I didn't create a tracking issue so please feel free to comment here.)

## Problem

- The `ZR` feature makes it more obvious that we need some sort of flag so that an `ExitPre` / `QuitPre` / `VimLeave` handler can handle restarts differently than a normal exit. For example, it's common that users want `:mksession` on restart, but perhaps not on a normal exit.
- Nvim has no way to report its "uptime".
- Lack of the above also makes test coverage for restart somewhat awkward.

## Solution

- Introduce `v:starttime`
- Introduce `v:exitreason`
- ~~Introduce `v:startreason`~~

comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -268,10 +268,25 @@ M.vars = {
       Exit code, or |v:null| before invoking the |VimLeavePre|
       and |VimLeave| autocmds.  See |:q|, |:x| and |:cquit|.
       Example: >vim
-        :au VimLeave * echo "Exit value is " .. v:exiting
+        :au VimLeave * echo "Exit code is " .. v:exiting
       <
     ]=],
   },
+  exitreason = {
+    type = 'string',
+    desc = [=[
+      Reason for the current exit. Set before |QuitPre| is triggered.
+      Possible values:
+      - ""          Not exiting.
+      - "quit"      |:quit|, |:qall|, |:wq|, |ZZ|, |ZQ|, etc.
+      - "restart"   |:restart|, |ZR|.

comment_data.body: probably should drop this and use `assert_new_pid`, or combine them, but that's too much churn for now.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -207,22 +207,24 @@ describe('TUI :restart', function()
   before_each(n.clear)
   after_each(n.check_close)
 
-  ---@param exp boolean Restart expected
+  --- Asserts that the server at `addr` has (or has not) restarted since `starttime`.
+  ---@param starttime integer v:starttime of the server before restart
   ---@param sess table Session
   ---@param addr string
-  local function assert_restarted(exp, sess, addr)
-    local s = sess
+  ---@return integer starttime
+  ---@return table session
+  local function assert_restarted(starttime, sess, addr)

comment_data.body: Should mention that the timestamp is monotonic, like in `news.txt`.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -618,6 +633,15 @@ v:stacktrace
 		stack trace.  See also |v:exception|, |v:throwpoint|, and
 		|throw-variables|.
 
+				*v:starttime* *starttime-variable*
+v:starttime
+		Timestamp (in nanoseconds) when the Nvim process started.

comment_data.body: This is good stuff! Makes me think we might want an `:uptime` command eventually.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -618,6 +633,16 @@ v:stacktrace
 		stack trace.  See also |v:exception|, |v:throwpoint|, and
 		|throw-variables|.
 
+				*v:starttime* *starttime-variable*
+v:starttime
+		Timestamp (monotonic nanoseconds) when the Nvim process
+		started.
+
+		To see the current "uptime": >lua
+		  vim.print(('uptime: %d seconds'):format((vim.uv.hrtime() - vim.v.starttime) / 1e9))

comment_data.body: indeed :)) or toss into `:version` or something, initially
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -618,6 +633,16 @@ v:stacktrace
 		stack trace.  See also |v:exception|, |v:throwpoint|, and
 		|throw-variables|.
 
+				*v:starttime* *starttime-variable*
+v:starttime
+		Timestamp (monotonic nanoseconds) when the Nvim process
+		started.
+
+		To see the current "uptime": >lua
+		  vim.print(('uptime: %d seconds'):format((vim.uv.hrtime() - vim.v.starttime) / 1e9))

comment_data.body: I might implement `:uptime`, then. I've been wanting to make a new excmd now that you've refactored the C side of it so nicely.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -618,6 +633,16 @@ v:stacktrace
 		stack trace.  See also |v:exception|, |v:throwpoint|, and
 		|throw-variables|.
 
+				*v:starttime* *starttime-variable*

comment_regular_data.body: Is `v:startreason` planned for later? I think it would be useful to know if Nvim started due to a call to `:restart`.

> Is `v:startreason` planned for later? I think it would be useful to know if Nvim started due to a call to `:restart`.

Waiting to see if it's actually needed. It can be achieved by `:restart let g:startreason='restart'`. Though awkward with `ZR`.

Backport failed for `release-0.12`, because it was unable to cherry-pick the commit(s).

Please cherry-pick the changes locally and resolve any conflicts.
```bash
git fetch origin release-0.12
git worktree add -d .worktree/backport-39282-to-release-0.12 origin/release-0.12
cd .worktree/backport-39282-to-release-0.12
git switch --create backport-39282-to-release-0.12
git cherry-pick -x 28ba06837281e14a7dc5ade143642d11475d2823
```

title: feat(:restart): v:starttime, v:exitreason
